message:
feat(abi): expand process family ABI coverage with 16 new functions

Add 16 new process/credential ABI functions:

Process group/session (unistd_abi):
- getpgid, setpgid, getsid, setsid

Credential operations (unistd_abi):
- setuid, seteuid, setreuid (user ID)
- setgid, setegid, setregid (group ID)
- getgroups, setgroups (supplementary groups)

Extended wait (process_abi):
- wait3 (delegates to wait4)
- wait4 (SYS_wait4 with rusage)
- waitid (SYS_waitid with siginfo)
- vfork (delegates to fork on modern Linux)

All functions follow RawSyscall pattern with membrane gating.
Support matrix: 307 → 323 symbols total.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2vv.5","title":"Symbol expansion: socket/network family","description":"Complete socket/network family coverage.

Current State:
- Basic socket ops: likely RawSyscall
- Address functions (inet_pton, inet_ntop): partial Implemented
- Resolver (getaddrinfo, getnameinfo): GlibcCallThrough

Target Functions:
Socket operations (most are RawSyscall):
- socket, bind, listen, accept, connect
- send, sendto, sendmsg, recv, recvfrom, recvmsg
- shutdown, getsockname, getpeername
- setsockopt, getsockopt
- socketpair

Address conversion:
- inet_aton, inet_addr, inet_ntoa (legacy IPv4)
- inet_pton, inet_ntop (modern)
- htonl, htons, ntohl, ntohs (byte order)

Name resolution (requires NSS):
- getaddrinfo, freeaddrinfo, gai_strerror
- getnameinfo
- gethostbyname, gethostbyaddr (legacy)
- getservbyname, getservbyport

Implementation Notes:
- Socket syscalls are straightforward RawSyscall
- Address functions are pure computation (safe Rust)
- Resolver requires NSS infrastructure (dependency on bd-1rf)

Testing:
- Unit: address conversion edge cases
- Integration: simple client/server socket pairs
- E2E: curl-style HTTP request
- Hardened: invalid address buffer handling

Success Criteria:
- All socket.h/netinet/in.h functions classified
- Simple TCP/UDP programs work
- Resolver returns correct addresses

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:40.935379292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T21:37:05.873282694Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T21:37:05.873195180Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","socket"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.5","depends_on_id":"bd-25n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:48.963853366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:49.071978207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:40.935379292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":441,"issue_id":"bd-2vv.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Increment: Added 6 new socket/network ABI exports. New: socketpair, sendmsg, recvmsg, accept4 (RawSyscall), inet_aton, inet_ntoa (Implemented). libc.map now exports 18 socket + 9 inet symbols. Support matrix 280->286. All quality gates pass.","created_at":"2026-02-17T21:20:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vv.6","title":"Symbol expansion: time family with timezone","description":"Complete time family coverage with timezone support.

Current State:
- Basic time (time, gettimeofday): likely RawSyscall
- Clock functions (clock_gettime): RawSyscall
- Conversion (localtime, gmtime): partial
- Formatting (strftime, strptime): partial
- Timer functions: likely stubs

Target Functions:
Time retrieval:
- time, gettimeofday, clock_gettime, clock_getres

Conversion:
- localtime, localtime_r, gmtime, gmtime_r
- mktime, timegm
- asctime, asctime_r, ctime, ctime_r

Formatting:
- strftime (complex - locale-dependent)
- strptime (parsing - complex)

Timers:
- timer_create, timer_settime, timer_gettime, timer_delete
- setitimer, getitimer

Sleep:
- sleep, usleep, nanosleep, clock_nanosleep

Timezone:
- tzset, tzname, timezone, daylight

Implementation Notes:
- Time syscalls are straightforward
- Conversion requires timezone database handling
- strftime is locale-dependent (needs locale infrastructure)
- Timers require signal integration

Complexity:
Timezone handling is surprisingly complex:
- TZ environment variable parsing
- /etc/localtime reading
- Leap second handling
- Historical timezone changes

Testing:
- Unit: conversion round-trips
- Unit: DST boundary handling
- Integration: strftime with various format strings
- E2E: date command equivalent

Success Criteria:
- All time.h functions classified Implemented
- Timezone conversions match glibc
- strftime output matches glibc format

## Acceptance Criteria

All quality gates pass: cargo check, clippy, fmt, 18/18 time tests, drift test.","created_at":"2026-02-17T21:47:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vv.7","title":"Symbol expansion: signal family with safety","description":"Complete signal family coverage with async-signal-safety.

Current State:
- signal, sigaction: likely partial
- sigprocmask: likely RawSyscall
- raise, kill: likely RawSyscall
- sigsetjmp/siglongjmp: requires setjmp (bd-2ry)

Target Functions:
Basic signal handling:
- signal, sigaction
- sigprocmask, pthread_sigmask
- sigemptyset, sigfillset, sigaddset, sigdelset, sigismember

Signal delivery:
- raise, kill, killpg
- sigqueue (with value)

Signal waiting:
- pause, sigsuspend, sigwait, sigwaitinfo, sigtimedwait

Stack:
- sigaltstack

Non-local jump:
- sigsetjmp, siglongjmp (depends on setjmp impl)

Implementation Notes:
Signal handling is security-critical:
- Async-signal-safety requirements (can't call malloc in handler)
- Signal frame layout is architecture-specific
- SA_SIGINFO vs traditional handler signatures

TSM Considerations:
- Signal handlers run in restricted context
- Membrane must be async-signal-safe
- Validation in signal context must be fast/simple

Testing:
- Unit: signal mask operations
- Integration: signal handler registration and delivery
- Concurrency: signals + threads interaction
- E2E: Ctrl-C handling, SIGTERM graceful shutdown

Success Criteria:
- All signal.h functions classified
- Signal handlers work correctly
- No deadlocks in signal + membrane interaction

## Acceptance Criteria

All quality gates pass: cargo check, clippy, fmt, signal tests (17/17), drift test.","created_at":"2026-02-17T21:36:45Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2vv.8","title":"Symbol expansion: process family","description":"Complete process family coverage.

Current State:
- fork, execve: likely RawSyscall
- wait, waitpid: likely RawSyscall
- getpid, getppid, getuid, etc.: RawSyscall
- system, popen: likely stubs

Target Functions:
Process creation:
- fork, vfork (deprecated but used)
- execve, execl, execle, execlp, execv, execvp, execvpe
- posix_spawn, posix_spawnp

Process waiting:
- wait, waitpid, wait3, wait4
- waitid

Process IDs:
- getpid, getppid, getpgid, getsid
- setpgid, setsid

User/Group IDs:
- getuid, geteuid, getgid, getegid
- setuid, seteuid, setreuid, setresuid
- setgid, setegid, setregid, setresgid
- getgroups, setgroups

High-level:
- system (fork + exec + wait)
- popen, pclose (fork + exec + pipe)

Implementation Notes:
- Most process syscalls are RawSyscall
- fork requires careful membrane state handling
- exec replaces address space (membrane cleanup)
- system/popen need shell invocation

TSM Considerations:
- fork: child inherits membrane state (generation counters)
- exec: membrane cleanup before exec
- Credential operations are security-sensitive

Testing:
- Unit: ID operations
- Integration: fork + exec + wait sequences
- E2E: shell command execution via system()
- Security: credential change validation

Success Criteria:
- All unistd.h process functions classified
- fork/exec sequences work correctly
- Credential operations work

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-2vv.9","title":"Full symbol-universe normalization + support classification pipeline","description":"Background:
- Closing symbol gaps requires a canonical source for the full target symbol universe and support taxonomy.

Scope:
- Import and normalize full target symbol universe, then classify every symbol with current support state and confidence.
- Mark unknown/unverified symbols explicitly.

Deliverables:
1) Normalized symbol universe dataset.
2) Classification pipeline into support matrix.
3) Unknown/unverified symbol action list.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every target symbol has a non-ambiguous classification state.
- Classification output is reproducible and diff-friendly.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for symbol normalization/classification rules.
- E2E rebuild of support matrix from source data.
- Logs: trace_id, symbol, family, classification, confidence.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:28.409676482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T21:27:21.347523879Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T21:27:21.347441665Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","support-matrix","symbols"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:28.409676482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2wp","title":"Perf baseline suite: hyperfine + criterion for strict/hardened critical paths","description":"Extreme optimization mapping.

Deliverables:
- Define canonical benchmark commands for allocator, string hot paths, and membrane decision path.
- Capture p50/p95/p99 baseline snapshots per mode.

Acceptance:
- Baselines regenerate deterministically in CI-capable environment.
- Regressions are detectable with clear thresholds.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): perf_baseline_test (8 pass) + gate script. Enforced suites covered, regression thresholds match budget policy, regeneration procedure complete.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:11.789015856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","close_reason":"Perf baseline suite operational. 8 harness tests pass. check_perf_baseline.sh validates baseline coverage, benchmark suites, percentile targets, and regression thresholds.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline","critique","perf"]}

@@ -17,19 +17,19 @@ FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened LD_PRELOAD=/usr/lib/frankenlibc/libfrankenlibc_abi.so
 ## Current Implementation Reality (Machine-Generated)
 
 Source of truth: `tests/conformance/reality_report.v1.json` (generated `2026-02-13T18:07:59Z`).
-Reality snapshot: total_exported=307, implemented=202, raw_syscall=97, glibc_call_through=8, stub=0.
+Reality snapshot: total_exported=323, implemented=202, raw_syscall=113, glibc_call_through=8, stub=0.
 Counts below reflect that generated snapshot and will change as matrix drift fixes land.
 
 Current implementation is **hybrid interposition**, not full replacement. Exported symbols are classified into four support-taxonomy states:
 
 | Status | Count | Share | Meaning |
